text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to software development that focuses on flexibility and collaboration. Work is divided into short cycles called sprints, usually lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint begins with a planning meeting where the team selects tasks from a backlog, which is a list of pending work. These tasks are often described as user stories, short explanations of a feature from the user's perspective. Every day, the team holds a brief standup meeting to discuss progress and any problems. This process is called iterative development, as the team repeats the cycle, continuously improving the product based on feedback.
